population: Septic patients (sepsis-3 criteria) admitted to ICU on day 1
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
predictor: Sepsis Mortality Risk Score (SMRS) - developed using LASSO technique
method: LASSO variable selection, logistic regression model, ROC analysis, calibration slope, Brier score, decision curve analysis
effect_size: Risk stratification: low (3.2%), moderate (12.4%), high (30.7%), very high (68.1%) in development; 2.8%, 10.5%, 21.1%, 51.2% in validation
performance: AUC 0.789 (development), AUC 0.765 (validation); calibration slope 0.862, Brier value 0.140 (validation); positive net benefit on decision curve analysis
notes: Development cohort from MIMIC III database; external validation from eICU database. 30-day mortality was 16.7% in development set and 20.1% in validation set. Authors note further modification and external validation required.

source_info.section: Kai Zhang[1†] , Shufang Zhang[2†] , Wei Cui , Yucai Hong , Gensheng Zhang * and Zhongheng Zhang *

population: Sepsis-3 patients in ICU
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: LASSO regression, machine learning models (MARS, XGBoost, and others)
notes: Study developed SMRS and four machine learning models to predict 30-day mortality in sepsis-3 patients. External validation performed using eICU database. Insufficient detail provided in the extracted text to report specific performance metrics.

population: Adult ICU patients meeting Sepsis-3 criteria from MIMIC III database
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: Prediction model development (TRIPOD guidelines)
notes: Development set from MIMIC III (N=5,443); validation set from eICU database (N=5,658, 1,042 deaths, mortality rate 20.1%). Variables with >20% missing data excluded.

population: Sepsis patients in development set
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
predictor: Sepsis Mortality Risk Score (SMRS) - 13-factor composite score (range 0-34)
method: LASSO regression model, ROC analysis, calibration (Brier score, calibration slope)
effect_size: Low risk (0-6 points): 3.2% mortality; Moderate risk (7-11 points): 12.4% mortality; High risk (12-14 points): 30.7% mortality; Very high risk (≥15 points): 68.1% mortality
notes: SMRS outperformed other severity scores in discrimination. VIF < 5 confirmed no significant multicollinearity. 35 features reduced to 15 via LASSO, then 13 entered final model.

population: Sepsis patients in external validation set
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis (AUC), calibration slope, Brier score, Decision Curve Analysis (DCA)
performance: AUC 0.765; SMRS vs. APACHE IV: P=0.221; SMRS vs. SAPS II: P=0.177
notes: SMRS stratified patients into low (2.8%), moderate (10.5%), high (21.1%), and very high (51.2%) mortality risk groups. Net benefit of SMRS comparable to SAPS II and APACHE IV and better than SOFA between threshold probabilities of 10-80%.

population: Sepsis patients in external validation set
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis (AUC)
notes: Compared to SMRS (AUC 0.765); no statistically significant difference (P=0.221).

population: Sepsis patients in external validation set
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis (AUC)
notes: Compared to SMRS (AUC 0.765); no statistically significant difference (P=0.177).

population: Sepsis patients
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis (AUC)
performance: AUC > 0.8 in development set; best performance among four models in development and validation sets
notes: Other models tested included logistic regression (AUC ≤ 0.8). SMRS risk categories: low, moderate, high, very high with mortality rates 0%, 10.5%, 21.1%, 51.2% respectively.

population: ICU patients with sepsis (Sepsis-3 criteria)
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: Scoring system with LASSO regression; ROC analysis
performance: Discrimination comparable to APACHE IV and SAPS II; significantly better than SOFA in validation set
notes: SMRS contains 13 simple variables recorded in clinical routines. Developed using Sepsis-3 criteria. Described as showing moderate performance in predicting 30-day mortality and risk-stratifying ICU patients with sepsis.

population: Sepsis-3 ICU patients
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis
performance: AUC reported in ROC curves for development and validation sets (values not explicitly stated in excerpt)
notes: SMRS constructed using LASSO method identifying 13 predictive features from 35 clinical variables; includes mechanical ventilation, serum lactate, hypothermia, hypotension, and advanced age

population: Sepsis-3 ICU patients
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: ROC analysis
notes: SOFA score had low discriminatory power for 30-day mortality prediction in this study's sepsis-3 population

population: Sepsis-3 ICU patients
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: Logistic regression / LASSO
effect_size: OR approximately 2 (two-fold increase in odds of death)
notes: Mechanical ventilation included as a predictor in the SMRS model

population: Sepsis-3 ICU patients
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: LASSO feature selection
effect_size: Assigned 3 or 6 points in final risk score
notes: Serum lactate identified as strong predictor of mortality; included in SMRS

population: ICU patients with sepsis
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: Decision curve analysis (DCA)
effect_size: 80% probability threshold considered sufficient for accurate mortality risk assessment
notes: SMRS demonstrated robust discrimination and calibration; compared against SAPS II, SOFA, and APACHE IV in development and validation sets

population: ICU patients with sepsis
cohort_age: Survivors: median 66.0 (IQR 53.0–78.0) years; Non-survivors: median 75.0 (IQR 61.0–84.0) years
method: Machine learning model comparison
notes: Machine learning techniques, particularly XGBoost, showed high potential in sepsis population mortality prediction
